summary.brief: Rolon, F. v. Davies, T.. Medical malpractice case involving oncology.

evidence_index.text: On April 30, 2010, Dr. Voystock, of Appellee Surgical Specialists of Lancaster, performed a hemicolectomy on Maria Sanchez-Rodriguez
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The hemicolectomy was an emergency surgery to address a bowel perforation that occurred during a routine colonoscopy.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On May 19, 2010, Decedent went to the LGH emergency room (“ER”) complaining of pain in her right lower back, right hip, and right leg.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The leg was swollen and blue. Appellee Trystan Davies, M.D., of Appellee Lancaster Emergency Associates, Ltd., examined Decedent in the ER.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Davies ordered an ultrasound to check for a blood clot, i.e. deep vein thrombosis (“DVT”), in Decedent’s lower leg.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Appellee Matthew Wiggins, M.D., of Appellee Lancaster Radiology Associates, Ltd. interpreted the results of the ultrasound to reveal no DVT in the lower right leg, but slow blood flow in some of the veins.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Voystock ordered an arterial study, and it revealed no evidence of arterial blockage.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Within one hour of discharge, Decedent collapsed in the elevator of her apartment building and was returned to the ER via ambulance.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: An autopsy revealed Decedent, age 61, died from a pulmonary embolism.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Q. Okay. Doctor, based on your review of the records and the deposition of Dr. Voystock and the other depositions in this case, did you come to an opinion to within a reasonable degree of medical certainty as to whether or not Dr. Voystock violated the standard of care in his care and treatment of this patient?
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: testified that, had Dr. Voystock adhered to the standard of care, Decedent’s
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: For all of these reasons, we will vacate the judgment in favor of Dr. Voystock and Surgical Specialists of Lancaster, P.C. and award Appellant a new trial against those parties only.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: We affirm the judgment in favor of all other defendants.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Pain in right lower back, right hip, and right leg
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient presented to the emergency room with pain in her right lower back, right hip, and right leg. The leg was swollen and blue.

focused_exam.right_leg: Swollen, blue, colder, and weaker pulse compared to the left leg

critical_findings: Colder and weaker pulse in right leg

requestables.name: Ultrasound of lower leg
requestables.description: Ultrasound to check for DVT in the lower leg

reveal.result_summary: No DVT in lower right leg, but slow blood flow in some veins
reveal.clinical_significance: Indicates possible issue higher up in the venous system
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt further testing to rule out pelvic DVT

requestables.name: Arterial study
requestables.description: Study to check for arterial blockage

reveal.clinical_significance: Rules out arterial cause for symptoms
reveal.decision_impact: Should have prompted consideration of venous issues

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to ER with acute symptoms

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in ER with symptoms suggestive of DVT
decision_points.prompt: What further investigation should be conducted after the ultrasound?

options.description: Order further imaging to check for pelvic DVT
options.clinical_reasoning: Symptoms and ultrasound findings suggest a possible pelvic DVT, which is more dangerous.

options.description: Discharge patient with no further testing
options.clinical_reasoning: Improvement in symptoms during bedrest was misinterpreted as readiness for discharge.

actual_action_defendant.description: Patient was discharged without further testing

expected_action_court.description: Further imaging should have been ordered to rule out pelvic DVT
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated the need to rule out pelvic DVT

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to rule out pelvic DVT despite suggestive symptoms and ultrasound findings
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Further imaging to confirm or rule out pelvic DVT
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient collapsed and died from a pulmonary embolism shortly after discharge

key_findings: Jury found in favor of defendants
Nonsuit granted for Dr. Voystock and Surgical Specialists of Lancaster

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to order further imaging for pelvic DVT
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Further imaging could have identified a pelvic DVT

factual_timeline.event: Patient presented to ER with leg pain and swelling
Patient collapsed and died from pulmonary embolism

tests_performed.result: No DVT, slow blood flow in some veins

allegations.allegation: Failure to diagnose and treat DVT

court_findings.finding: No liability found for remaining defendants
court_findings.legal_significance: Jury verdict in favor of defendants
